violations.text: Pasta, beef, and potatoes observed held at 47-48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were placed in freezer to rapidly chill. Discussed not overfilling containers in prep unit with staff. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025
Risotto, and bean salad observed marked with dates exceeding 7 days. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025
Uncovered containers of food observed in the walk in cooler. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (2 vegetable peelers, and food containers on clean storage rack) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items were taken to the warewashing area to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Bar items are to be cleaned and sanitized at the kitchen dishwasher until the bar dishwasher is functioning properly. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

Gravy, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Gravy was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has some of the original delivery plastic attached, which is fraying along the top. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

The ceiling above the dishwasher is in poor repair, with a dirty fish bucket lid propped over a cracked vent area. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a stack of containers on it. The hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
The hand-washing sink in the women's toilet room was lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2025
Shellstock tags did not have all of the required information. Dates of final use were lacking. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Open containers of food observed in freezer on the cooking line, with packaged food in contact with uncovered food. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered contaiers, or wrapping. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher at the bar was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives, and potato chopper ) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Potatoes, cream, chicken, cut tomatoes, observed held at 44-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cream was disposed of. Foods were placed in the freezer to rapidly chill. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Beef observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
Bolognese, and bean salad observed with date marks exceeding 7 days. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2025

Thermometers are lacking in some refrigeration units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
The hand sink next to the food prep area near the ice machine needs a splash guard. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
Celery were not washed before being cut. Raw fruits and vegetables must be thoroughly washed in water to remove soil and other contaminants before they are cut. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Utensils in the cooking area observed stored on an unclean surface (beneath grill) between uses.The ice scoop and other bar utensils observed stored in stagnant water. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Scoops observed in containers of dry ingredients with the handles in the food. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Water observed in the food processor bowl, and in numerous food containers stacked on the clean storage rack.After cleaning & sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the areas of the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Pressed fiberboard is used in the construction of shelves in the kitchen area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2025

A board in poor repair is used as a food prep counter in the area near the ice machine. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:knife rackutensil containers at the food runner areakitchen shelves, including the undersideblender basecutting board shelf has an accumulation of pooled liquid and debris New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in areas of the kitchen. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
The wall near the handsink is in poor repair, with holes, and the FRP pulling away from the wall. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in wall nook near cooking area, and on dry storage shelf. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/01/2023

The coving by the icemachine and dishroom were observed poor repair. The floor tiles in the dishroom were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2023
No person in charge or person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023

The hand-washing sink at the bar was blocked by miscellaneous bar tools. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination (Raw shelled eggs over mussels). Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black and pink residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023

Milk and Half and Half were observed held at 59 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Milk and Half and Half were voluntarily disposed. Service station lowboy was observed at an ambient of 57 degrees Fahrenheit. No potentially hazardous foods to be stored in unit until unit is repaired and can maintain an ambient of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023

Various prepared foods in the walk-in cooler were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023

Working containers of sauce bottles and dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2023

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machines. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: 1. gaskets of prep units on the line 2. interior of prep units 3. left side of oven 4. speed racks by ice machine New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023

The following areas have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris: 1. Kitchen hoods and vents 2. Floors underneath shelving in walk-in cooler 3. Floors underneath prep units and cookline 4. Floors in the bar area The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023

Coving by the ice machine and in the dishroom were observed in poor repair. Floor tiles in the dishroom were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2023
